projects.title: Dakar Surf Report
projects.pitch: Daily swell, wind, and tide forecasts for every break from Ngor to Ouakam.
projects.description:
Surfers here rely on word of mouth and generic global forecast apps that don't know the local breaks. I want a lightweight PWA that combines open marine data with community reports from people actually in the water.

I've got the data pipeline sketched and a rough prototype pulling from Open-Meteo. Need help on the frontend and someone who knows the spots to validate the break profiles.

projects.title: Wolof Flash
projects.pitch: Spaced-repetition flashcards for learning Wolof, built with native speaker audio.
projects.description:
There's almost no good digital material for learning Wolof. Wolof Flash is a mobile-first flashcard app with real audio from native speakers, spaced repetition, and phrase packs organized around actual daily life — markets, taxis, greetings, family.

The SRS engine works. What's missing: more recorded audio, illustration for the phrase packs, and someone to help with Android polish.

projects.title: Open Repair Atlas
projects.pitch: A crowd-sourced map of repair cafés, spare-part shops, and fixers near you.
projects.description:
Throwing things away should be the last resort. The Repair Atlas maps every place that can fix your stuff — repair cafés, phone fixers, tailors, cobblers, electronics benches — with opening hours and what they repair.

Still at the idea stage. Looking for a co-founder type who cares about the circular economy, plus map/geo experience.

projects.title: Pocket Loom
projects.pitch: An open-source hardware kit that teaches weaving and binary logic at the same time.
projects.description:
A loom is the original programmable machine. Pocket Loom is a palm-sized, laser-cut weaving kit with punch cards — kids weave real patterns while learning how binary instructions work.

The v2 prototype weaves. Now it needs a proper assembly guide, curriculum worksheets, and someone with CAD skills to make it cheaper to cut.

projects.title: Teranga Tables
projects.pitch: Match travelers with local families for a real home-cooked meal.
projects.description:
The best meal you'll ever have while traveling is in someone's home. Teranga Tables lets host families list a weekly table and lets visitors book a seat — thieboudienne with the people who actually make it.

Needs everything: brand, booking flow, host onboarding. I bring the host network — twelve families already interested.

projects.title: Ledger of Small Wins
projects.pitch: A tiny journal app where entries can only be victories — no venting allowed.
projects.description:
A deliberately constrained journal: one entry per day, 140 characters max, and it has to be a win. The constraint is the product. Monthly recap generates a printable 'ledger page'.

Design direction is set (letterpress meets terminal). Need a frontend dev who cares about micro-interactions and someone to build the recap/PDF generation.

projects.title: Minibus Quest
projects.pitch: A cozy management game about running a car rapide route through a growing city.
projects.description:
Route planning, colorful bus livery customization, regulars with their own stories, and the eternal battle with traffic. Think Mini Motorways meets a Dakar car rapide.

Playable prototype exists in Godot. Looking for a pixel artist and a sound person — the horn sounds alone deserve a specialist.

projects.title: Harmattan Watch
projects.pitch: Hyperlocal air-quality alerts from a mesh of cheap DIY dust sensors.
projects.description:
Dust season affects everyone's health but the data is coarse. Harmattan Watch is a network of $25 particulate sensors people mount on balconies, feeding a shared live map with SMS alerts for sensitive groups.

Three sensors are live on my street. Need firmware help, someone for the data pipeline, and community organizers to grow the mesh.

projects.title: Fieldnotes FM
projects.pitch: A micro-podcast platform where episodes are capped at five minutes, recorded outside.
projects.description:
One rule: record outdoors. Street markets, bird calls, rain on tin roofs, an interview on a park bench. Five minutes max. Fieldnotes FM is podcasting stripped back to sound and place.

At the idea stage with a name and far too many opinions. Need an audio engineer to define the recording spec and a developer for upload + playback.

joinRequests.name: Yacine T.
joinRequests.message: I surf Ouakam most weekends and build React apps most weekdays. Happy to own the frontend.

joinRequests.name: Sofia L.
joinRequests.message: Product designer, ex-weather-app. I have strong opinions about how forecasts should look on a phone in bright sunlight.

joinRequests.name: Ken W.
joinRequests.message: I run a small maker space and can prototype cut files quickly. The punch card idea is brilliant.

joinRequests.name: Awa G.
joinRequests.message: Pixel artist, portfolio full of tiny vehicles. Car rapide liveries are a dream brief.

joinRequests.name: Daniel O.
joinRequests.message: Data engineer with sensor pipeline experience. Can set up ingestion and dashboards this month.
